name: Agidi / Eko
intro: Light and jelly-like, Eko is made using white corn. It is very bland on its own and is often served alongside soups like efo riro or pepper soup, or with akara or moin moin. The combination of cold eko and a steaming hot accompaniment makes for a unique taste experience.
method: Place the corn flour and water in a saucepan and mix well. Cook over medium heat, stirring continuously, until the ogi thickens, becomes silky smooth and starts to bubble on top. This should take 10-12 minutes.
Pour the mixture evenly into 2 uma/banana leaf parcels or small bowls/ramekins. Leave to cool down completely for at least 2 hours before unwrapping/unmolding.
Serve cold with hot moin moin, akara or a soup of your choice such as pepper soup or efo riro.
